name: Pork and Shrimp Lumpia
recipeIngredient: 1/3 lb Med. size raw shrimp, shelled, deveined, and chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak the dried mushrooms in warm water to cover for 30 minutes, then drain.
2. Cut off and discard the stems; finely chop the caps.
3. Crumble the pork into a wide frying pan. Add onions and garlic and cook over medium-high heat, stirring until the meat is browned (about 6 minutes).
4. Add shrimp, mushrooms, and water chestnuts and cook for 2 minutes.
5. Stir in soy sauce. Let cool; then discard excess pan juices.
6. Fill and fold lumpia, starting from a corner, rolling up into diamond shapes, folding the sides in and moistening the wrapper edges with egg to seal. (You may cover and refrigerate for up to 8 hours at this point.)
7. Deep fry about 4-5 lumpia at a time at 340°F in a deep pan for 2-3 minutes.
8. Drain on paper towels.
9. Serve with sweet & sour sauce.
